kingdomFocus: Getting people to know who the Kingdom is
kingdomAvoidUnlessNeeded: Tasks that create no visible movement
Generic content production without a real-world purpose
currentProfile.decisionStyle: Trust-centered, destination-oriented consensus. Percival does not seek control for its own sake and is comfortable letting Levon and Thomas sponsor major direction when trust, mission alignment, and Kingdom benefit are intact. He is more likely to object when a decision damages trust, drifts from the mission, lacks an execution owner, or turns into planning without movement. He prefers real conversation over formal process and wants to be understood before being challenged.
strengths: Helping others become more productive when he is actively involved
Converting abstract ideas into tangible action
Believing in the destination even when the route changes
weakSpots: Tasks that feel detached from real-world action
Being forced to communicate complex thoughts quickly without being heard
preferredInputs: Examples of what the finished thing should look like
A simple explanation of what needs to be done and how
preferredOutputs: Explain what needs to be done
Use route logic: destination, current location, route, roadblocks, checkpoints, reroute rules, arrival proof
Give a visible model or example before asking for execution
Separate what is known from what is assumed
Translate strategy into something he can physically or practically do
approvalCriteria: The current starting point is verified
Visible motion exists or can begin quickly
The decision helps the Kingdom move forward
The plan does not create unnecessary complexity
The plan does not require Percival to own work he strongly avoids unless explicitly accepted
blockingCriteria: Taking on more work when nobody will execute it
Generic strategy with no real-world path
evidenceRequiredForApproval: Proof that the work can be acted on
Proof that the work protects or increases trust
A practical example or visible model
Arrival proof that defines how success will be recognized
questionsTheyAlwaysAsk: Who is going to execute this?
How does this help the Kingdom?
Does this align with the mission?
What does this look like in the real world?
What are we actually trying to get done?
Can someone start doing this now?
Are we taking on more than we can execute?
Is this just planning or is this movement?
Are the guys aligned on this?
thingsTheyDoNotWantAIToDo: Replace human Knight authority with AI authority
Treat the profile as permanent truth
currentProfile.communicationStyle: Direct, conversational, practical, example-driven, and grounded in real-world execution. Percival prefers to talk things through when alignment matters. He wants Merlin and Albion to listen first, understand the objection, then challenge if needed. He responds best to concrete examples, visible models, and clear next steps rather than polished consultant language.
requiresFullRoundtableApprovalFor: Any decision where a Knight objects
Any decision that creates mission concerns
Any decision that creates trust concerns
Any decision that could damage community confidence
Any decision where consensus has broken down
Any decision that changes governance authority
Any decision where AI attempts to approve final authority
Any decision with unclear ownership that still creates major obligation
Any decision that risks the Kingdom's long-term identity
defersToOtherKnightsWhen: Figuring out niche human or market positioning goes to Levon
Translating ideas into language other people understand goes to Levon
AI buildout questions go to Thomas
Computer and software hiccups go to Thomas
escalationTriggers: A decision could change the Kingdom's identity
A decision creates major obligation without an operator
A route is proposed without a destination
Execution begins without verified current location
AI attempts to decide instead of advise
merlinPresentationRules: Show what the outcome looks like
Define the destination before proposing the route
Verify the current location before recommending execution
Identify who owns the next action
Keep the answer grounded in what can actually be done
Do not produce generic business advice
Do not flatter or agree just to agree
Make the path visible enough for monkey-see-monkey-do execution
albionRoutingRules: Allow route changes if destination remains intact
Do not execute without a destination
Do not execute without a verified starting point
Do not claim certainty without evidence
Identify traffic, roadblocks, risks, and missing data
Define reroute rules before blockers appear
Define arrival proof before declaring success
Escalate to the full Roundtable when any Knight blocks
Treat AI as advisor and organizer, never final approver
roundtableCollaborationRules: Live discussion is preferred over documents
A Knight objection triggers Roundtable review
Nothing major passes unless all three Knights approve
AI may advise, summarize, challenge, and organize
If two Knights agree and one disagrees, the group pauses for discussion
The disagreeing Knight must be heard and understood before persuasion
Disagreement should be worked through, not steamrolled
The Kingdom's mission outranks individual preference
The Knights may make major moves when trust is intact, but communication must follow quickly

highConfidence: Strong trust in Levon and Thomas
Percival owns merchandising and manufacturing of marketing and sales materials, including metal business cards, NFC cards, T-shirts, and related physical materials
AI making things up is unacceptable
Levon is trusted for communication, sales, and idea translation
Thomas is trusted for software, technology, systems, and AI buildout
Percival wants to be understood before being challenged
The Kingdom is a long-running mission, not a temporary project
Percival is willing to sacrifice heavily for the Kingdom
Visible motion matters more than theoretical planning
mediumConfidence: Acts as momentum amplifier for the Roundtable
Trust in the Knights is a primary approval mechanism
Percival will usually support Levon and Thomas when mission and trust are intact
Percival is more likely to block from mission or trust concerns than from personal control concerns
Percival is energized by tangible creation such as XTool work
Percival dislikes overbuilt systems without immediate use
Percival's role may be best understood as conviction engine plus operator
lowConfidence: Primary governance role is trust stewardship
Percival will rarely block if Levon and Thomas are aligned
Money concerns are the most likely remaining reason for hesitation after all other criteria are met
Percival's unique contribution is primarily increasing the productivity of the other Knights
needsValidationThroughDecisions: Conflict resolution behavior under high-stakes disagreement
Actual veto behavior when mission and trust are not at risk
How often money concerns override otherwise strong alignment
Whether Percival consistently avoids all clerical work or only low-context clerical work
Whether Percival should be treated as primary trust steward or shared trust steward
Whether Percival's operating role should be defined as execution owner, momentum owner, or mission owner
How Percival behaves when Levon and Thomas disagree with each other and he must mediate
How much post-decision notification is enough when Levon and Thomas sponsor major moves

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival self-identifies his natural push as Operations.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added Operations to selfDefinedPrimaryLanes.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival says he is best at understanding people.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added Understanding people to strengths.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival initially says there are no decisions he personally needs to approve because he trusts Levon and Thomas completely.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added high delegation and trust-centered decision style.

observedPatterns.pattern: Mission misalignment and trust damage are primary veto triggers.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added mission alignment and trust preservation to blockingCriteria and approvalCriteria.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival requires team consensus, market validation, plan understanding, and an execution owner before taking on important work.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added evidenceRequiredForApproval and approvalCriteria entries for consensus, validation, clear plan, and owner.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival's current focus is business growth, brand awareness, revenue, food trucks, and community alignment.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added business growth and ecosystem growth to kingdomFocus.

observedPatterns.pattern: AI loses Percival's trust when it makes things up, blindly agrees, or gives generic answers.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added AI trust failure patterns to thingsTheyDoNotWantAIToDo.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival moves fastest when shown what needs to be done, how it should be done, and what the result should look like.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added example-driven and practical output rules.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival is comfortable with Levon and Thomas making major directional decisions as long as they communicate afterward and stay aligned with Kingdom benefit.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added canSponsorEarlyWorkFor and trust-centered consensus logic.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival defers to Levon for talking to people, sales, communicating ideas, and niche human positioning.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added Levon-specific deference rules.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival defers to Thomas for software, computers, technology, systems, and technical hiccups.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added Thomas-specific deference rules.

observedPatterns.pattern: When one Knight disagrees, Percival expects immediate Roundtable discussion and live conversation until understanding and consensus emerge.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added escalationTriggers and roundtableCollaborationRules.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival believes the group gets more done when he is actively involved, even if others perform more visible productivity.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added momentum amplifier as medium confidence and needsValidationThroughDecisions.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival avoids repetitive clerical work, writing, calling, and phone-based outreach.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added to weakSpots and kingdomAvoidUnlessNeeded.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival is energized by tangible building and hands-on work, using the XTool example.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added hands-on execution and tangible creation to strengths and medium confidence.

observedPatterns.pattern: Merlin should not make assumptions, talk like a consultant, or fail to make work real-world and example-driven.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added Merlin presentation rules.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival avoids calling people, clerical work, screenshots, and Facebook sorting, but does not avoid in-person relationship work when paired with Levon.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Separated relationship orientation from cold outreach avoidance.

observedPatterns.pattern: If mission, trust, Levon, Thomas, validation, and execution owner all align, Percival struggles to imagine saying not yet except possibly for money issues.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added money hesitation as low confidence validation item.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival wants the Kingdom to remember that he would give up everything for it and believes it will eventually become number one if the work continues.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added long-term conviction, sacrifice, and Kingdom commitment to strengths and high confidence.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival prioritizes reaching the destination over preserving a specific route.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added destination orientation and reroute flexibility.

observedPatterns.pattern: If Percival dislikes a plan, he wants Merlin to understand his reasoning before challenging him.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added listen-before-challenge rule and dismissal as trust-loss trigger.

observedPatterns.pattern: Percival sees the Kingdom as something he has been trying to build his whole life, with Levon and Thomas now implementing complementary parts.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added long-running mission context and Kingdom relationship.

observedPatterns.pattern: Historical TradeScout and MealScout behavior shows repeated focus on trust architecture, community, free information, local ecosystem building, and monetizing acceleration rather than access.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Raised Trust stewardship and Trust architecture in profile.

observedPatterns.pattern: Historical behavior shows Percival repeatedly pulls ideas toward field testing, uploading real locations, talking to real vendors, generating content, and visible business motion.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Added visible motion, real-world testing, and planning-without-execution concerns.

observedPatterns.pattern: Historical behavior confirms hands-on learning through XTool, MealScout, TradeScout, and repeated practical experiments.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Raised hands-on learning and real-world examples to high confidence.

observedPatterns.pattern: Historical behavior suggests Percival's identity is broader than Operations: mission-driven operator, conviction engine, and trust steward.
observedPatterns.profileImpact: Expanded selfDefinedPrimaryLanes and decisionStyle.

profileUpdateRules: Do not update from a single weak signal unless the Knight explicitly confirms it.
Do update from repeated decision behavior.
Every update must include sourceRunId or sourceDecisionId.
Never overwrite prior profile history; append versioned changes.
When self-description and observed behavior conflict, preserve both and mark the conflict for validation.
Do not treat this profile as fixed role assignment.
Do not silently rewrite the profile.
Every future update must cite the decision, correction, approval, block, deferral, repeated pattern, or format rejection that caused it.
AI cannot approve governance changes; AI may only recommend profile updates for Knight review.

activitySourcesUsed.description: TradeScout doctrine repeatedly defined around free information, trust as currency, never selling leads, and monetizing acceleration rather than access.
MealScout planning repeatedly focused on food trucks, hosts, field work, content, real locations, vendor conversations, and visible traction.
Community Builder and Trade Partner discussions showed interest in local trust networks, business growth, partnerships, and revenue through aligned services.
XTool conversations showed hands-on learning, practical troubleshooting, tangible creation, and rapid execution when given a physical or visual task.
Repeated project conversations showed preference for moving from abstract plans into field testing, content, outreach, uploaded data, and execution ownership.
usedFor: Planning without movement as a blocker

activitySourcesUsed.description: Direct onboarding interview answers from Percival about operations, people understanding, trust in Levon and Thomas, blockers, evidence, AI trust, output preferences, deference, escalation, weak spots, destination orientation, and Kingdom identity.
